modal.fromEvents: From events tagged as violence against civilians or with civilian impact
modal.dataSourced: Data sourced from Al Jazeera, BBC, Reuters, CNN, AP, and other verified outlets. Figures represent individually attributed events and may undercount actual totals.
modal.figuresNote: Note: These figures reflect events with identified civilian impact. Actual civilian toll is significantly higher.
modal.noEventsIn24h: No events in the last 24 hours.

ask.askAnything: Ask anything about the conflict. Powered by AI with
ask.disclaimerText: Responses are generated by AI and may contain errors, outdated information, or inaccuracies. Always verify critical information against primary sources.
ask.answersMayTake: answers may take a few seconds
ask.aiGenerated: AI-generated — may be inaccurate. Verify against primary sources.
ask.rateLimitReached: Rate limit reached. You can ask up to 10 questions per hour. Please wait a bit before asking again.
ask.q1: What is the current military situation in the 2026 Iran war?
ask.q2: How did Operation Epic Fury start and what triggered it?
ask.q3: Which countries are involved in Operation Epic Fury and why?
ask.q4: What is happening at the Strait of Hormuz?
ask.q5: What are the civilian casualty figures in the Iran war so far?
ask.q6: How has the war affected global oil prices and energy markets?
ask.q7: What is the current situation in Lebanon and Hezbollah's involvement?
ask.q8: What role has Cyprus played in Operation Epic Fury?
ask.q9: Who is Hezbollah and what is their role in this conflict?
ask.q10: What are the Houthis doing in the Red Sea and Yemen?
ask.q11: What is Russia's role and position in the 2026 Middle East war?
ask.q12: What has happened to Iran's military capabilities since the war started?

landing.subtitle: 2026 Iran War — Live Conflict Tracker
landing.description: A real-time, interactive map of the 2026 US-Israel war on Iran (Operation Epic Fury). Every airstrike, missile launch, and development — sourced, verified, and plotted on a live map with an AI analyst you can ask questions.
landing.contentWarningText: This application contains real-time reports of armed conflict, including military strikes, civilian casualties, and displacement. All data is sourced from verified reporting.
landing.noNewEvents: No new events reported since your last visit

about.title: About War Library
about.whatIsText: War Library is an interactive, living record of the ongoing armed conflict across the Middle East. It is designed to help people understand what is happening — the who, what, where, when, and why — in a way that is more immediate and contextual than traditional news reporting.
about.whyExistsText: When conflict spreads across multiple countries and involves dozens of actors, it becomes nearly impossible to keep up through individual news articles alone. War Library aggregates, maps, and contextualizes events from verified sources so that anyone — not just analysts or journalists — can understand the full picture.
about.howDataText: Events are sourced from an automated news pipeline that ingests articles from NewsData.io, Google News RSS, and direct RSS feeds from major outlets (Al Jazeera, BBC, NYT, The Guardian, France 24, DW News, CNN, Reuters, and others). Each article is processed through Claude AI for structured event extraction. Every event is attributed to its original source with a confidence score.
about.limit1: Casualty figures are based on reported numbers and may be incomplete or revised. Fog of war is real.
about.limit2: Coordinates are approximate (city/site level), not precise strike locations.
about.limit3: Source perspectives vary. We include reporting from all sides but label state-affiliated media accordingly.
about.limit4: This is not a military intelligence tool. It is an educational resource built from open-source information.
about.editorialPolicyText: War Library presents factual data without editorial commentary. Event descriptions are written in neutral, factual language. Where claims conflict between parties, both perspectives are noted. Source attribution is mandatory — no event is displayed without a traceable source.
about.openSourceText: War Library is an open-source project. The code, data pipeline configuration, and methodology are publicly available for review, contribution, and accountability.
about.contactText: To report an error, suggest a correction, or provide feedback, reach out at john@midatlantic.ai. I take data accuracy seriously and will investigate all reports.
about.privacyIntro: War Library respects your privacy. Here is exactly what we collect and why:
about.privacyIp: IP addresses are used temporarily for rate limiting AI questions (10/hour). They are stored in server memory only and are lost on restart. We do not log or persist IP addresses.
about.privacyAi: Questions you ask are sent to Anthropic's Claude AI for processing. They are not stored by War Library. Anthropic's privacy policy applies to AI processing.
about.privacyStorage: Local storage is used in your browser to remember your last visit date and briefing preferences. No cookies are set for tracking.
about.privacyPush: Push notifications — if you enable breaking event alerts, your browser generates an anonymous push subscription token. We store only this token (no name, email, IP, or device info) to deliver alerts. You can unsubscribe at any time through your browser settings. Expired tokens are automatically removed.
about.privacyAnalytics: No third-party analytics trackers are used. No Google Analytics, no Facebook Pixel, no ad networks. We collect anonymous page-view counts locally to understand usage — no personal data is stored or shared.
about.privacyNoSale: No data is sold or shared with third parties. War Library has no commercial interest in your data.
about.termsIntro: War Library is provided as-is for informational and educational purposes. By using this site you agree to the following:
about.term1: This is not a military intelligence tool. Do not use it for operational military decisions.
about.term2: Casualty figures and event details are based on open-source reporting and may contain errors. Always verify critical information against primary sources.
about.term3: AI-generated answers may contain inaccuracies. They are analytical summaries, not authoritative statements.
about.term4: Abuse of the AI system (prompt injection, off-topic use, excessive requests) may result in your access being temporarily restricted.
about.term5: Donation links direct to independent humanitarian organizations. War Library does not collect, process, or handle any donations.

sources.title: Data Sources
sources.subtitle: Every data point in War Library is traceable to its source.
sources.howWeSource: How we source and verify data
sources.methodology: Events are ingested from multiple independent sources and cross-referenced. Each event displays its source, and where possible, a direct link to the original reporting. We distinguish between
sources.verifiedDesc: events (confirmed by ACLED or multiple independent sources),
sources.reportedDesc: events (from a single reputable news organization), and
sources.unconfirmedDesc: events (social media or single-source). Casualty figures are reported numbers and may be revised.

donate.title: Humanitarian Aid
donate.subtitle: Human cost of the conflict · Verified organizations
donate.disclaimer: War Library does not collect or process donations.
donate.disclaimerText: All links go directly to each organization's official donation page. We receive no commission, referral fees, or financial benefit of any kind. Verify the URL in your browser before submitting payment information.
donate.humanCostIntro: Following the escalation of hostilities on February 28, 2026, the humanitarian situation across the Middle East has deteriorated rapidly. Armed attacks involving airstrikes on Iran, retaliatory strikes on Israel and Arab Gulf states, and regional military operations have created an acute, multi-country crisis.
donate.childrenImpact: Children killed or injured across the region
donate.sourcesIntro: All humanitarian data on this page is sourced from the organizations and agencies listed below. Every figure includes its source and reporting date. Where figures conflict between sources, both are presented with attribution.
donate.aboutPageText1: War Library is a neutral conflict-tracking resource. 100% of any monetization proceeds from this site are directed toward humanitarian aid. The organizations listed above were selected because they are internationally recognized, independently rated, and have verified active operations in the current conflict zone. Donation URLs were verified on March 8, 2026. Humanitarian data last verified mid-March 2026.
donate.aboutPageText2: Inclusion on this page does not constitute an endorsement of any organization's political positions. These organizations were chosen for their humanitarian mandate, operational presence, and financial transparency. If you believe any information on this page is outdated or incorrect, reach out at john@midatlantic.ai.
donate.casualtyNote: * Casualty figures vary by source. The Iranian government reports 1,444 killed and 18,551 injured (civilian figures only). Independent monitor Hengaw reports 5,900 killed (595 civilian, 5,305 military) as of March 20. The government figure counts civilians only; Hengaw's figure includes both civilian and military casualties. Both are presented for transparency.
donate.figuresNote: Figures are from official and independent sources as cited. Numbers are preliminary and expected to change as the conflict continues. Last verified: mid-March 2026.
donate.hungerRisk: Additional people at risk of hunger
